4257 // Some intrinsics (like experimental_gc_statepoint) can be used in invoke
4258 // context, so it is handled in visitCallBase and we should trigger it.
4259 return visitCallBase(*II);
4260}
4261
4262// Fence instruction simplification
4263Instruction *InstCombinerImpl::visitFenceInst(FenceInst &FI) {
4264 auto *NFI = dyn_cast<FenceInst>(FI.getNextNode());
4265 // This check is solely here to handle arbitrary target-dependent syncscopes.
4266 // TODO: Can remove if does not matter in practice.
4267 if (NFI && FI.isIdenticalTo(NFI))
4268 return eraseInstFromFunction(FI);
4269
4270 // Returns true if FI1 is identical or stronger fence than FI2.
4271 auto isIdenticalOrStrongerFence = [](FenceInst *FI1, FenceInst *FI2) {
4272 auto FI1SyncScope = FI1->getSyncScopeID();
4273 // Consider same scope, where scope is global or single-thread.
4274 if (FI1SyncScope != FI2->getSyncScopeID() ||
4275 (FI1SyncScope != SyncScope::System &&
4276 FI1SyncScope != SyncScope::SingleThread))
4277 return false;
4278
4279 return isAtLeastOrStrongerThan(FI1->getOrdering(), FI2->getOrdering());
4280 };
4281 if (NFI && isIdenticalOrStrongerFence(NFI, &FI))
4282 return eraseInstFromFunction(FI);
4283
4284 if (auto *PFI = dyn_cast_or_null<FenceInst>(FI.getPrevNode()))
4285 if (isIdenticalOrStrongerFence(PFI, &FI))
4286 return eraseInstFromFunction(FI);
4287 return nullptr;
4288}
4289
4290// InvokeInst simplification
4291Instruction *InstCombinerImpl::visitInvokeInst(InvokeInst &II) {
4292 return visitCallBase(II);
4293}
4294
4295// CallBrInst simplification
4296Instruction *InstCombinerImpl::visitCallBrInst(CallBrInst &CBI) {
4297 return visitCallBase(CBI);
4298}
4299
4300static Value *optimizeModularFormat(CallInst *CI, IRBuilderBase &B) {
4301 if (!CI->hasFnAttr("modular-format"))
4302 return nullptr;
4303
4304 SmallVector<StringRef> Args(
4305 llvm::split(CI->getFnAttr("modular-format").getValueAsString(), ','));
4306 // TODO: Make use of the first two arguments
4307 unsigned FirstArgIdx;
4308 [[maybe_unused]] bool Error;
4309 Error = Args[2].getAsInteger(10, FirstArgIdx);
4310 assert(!Error && "invalid first arg index");
4311 --FirstArgIdx;
4312 StringRef FnName = Args[3];
4313 StringRef ImplName = Args[4];
4314 ArrayRef<StringRef> AllAspects = ArrayRef<StringRef>(Args).drop_front(5);
4315
4316 if (AllAspects.empty())
4317 return nullptr;
4318
4319 SmallVector<StringRef> NeededAspects;
4320 for (StringRef Aspect : AllAspects) {
4321 if (Aspect == "float") {
4322 if (llvm::any_of(
4323 llvm::make_range(std::next(CI->arg_begin(), FirstArgIdx),
4324 CI->arg_end()),
4325 [](Value *V) { return V->getType()->isFloatingPointTy(); }))
4326 NeededAspects.push_back("float");
4327 } else {
4328 // Unknown aspects are always considered to be needed.
4329 NeededAspects.push_back(Aspect);
4330 }
4331 }
4332
4333 if (NeededAspects.size() == AllAspects.size())
4334 return nullptr;
4335
4336 Module *M = CI->getModule();
4337 LLVMContext &Ctx = M->getContext();
4338 Function *Callee = CI->getCalledFunction();
4339 FunctionCallee ModularFn = M->getOrInsertFunction(
4340 FnName, Callee->getFunctionType(),
4341 Callee->getAttributes().removeFnAttribute(Ctx, "modular-format"));
4342 CallInst *New = cast<CallInst>(CI->clone());
4343 New->setCalledFunction(ModularFn);
4344 New->removeFnAttr("modular-format");
4345 B.Insert(New);
4346
4347 const auto ReferenceAspect = [&](StringRef Aspect) {
4348 SmallString<20> Name = ImplName;
4349 Name += '_';
4350 Name += Aspect;
4351 Function *RelocNoneFn =
4352 Intrinsic::getOrInsertDeclaration(M, Intrinsic::reloc_none);
4353 B.CreateCall(RelocNoneFn,
4354 {MetadataAsValue::get(Ctx, MDString::get(Ctx, Name))});
4355 };
4356
4357 llvm::sort(NeededAspects);
4358 for (StringRef Request : NeededAspects)
4359 ReferenceAspect(Request);
4360
4361 return New;
4362}
4363
4364Instruction *InstCombinerImpl::tryOptimizeCall(CallInst *CI) {
4365 if (!CI->getCalledFunction()) return nullptr;
4366
4367 // Skip optimizing notail and musttail calls so
4368 // LibCallSimplifier::optimizeCall doesn't have to preserve those invariants.
4369 // LibCallSimplifier::optimizeCall should try to preserve tail calls though.
4370 if (CI->isMustTailCall() || CI->isNoTailCall())
4371 return nullptr;
4372
4373 auto InstCombineRAUW = [this](Instruction *From, Value *With) {
4374 replaceInstUsesWith(*From, With);
4375 };
4376 auto InstCombineErase = [this](Instruction *I) {
4377 eraseInstFromFunction(*I);
4378 };
4379 LibCallSimplifier Simplifier(DL, &TLI, &DT, &DC, &AC, ORE, BFI, PSI,
4380 InstCombineRAUW, InstCombineErase);
4381 if (Value *With = Simplifier.optimizeCall(CI, Builder)) {
4382 ++NumSimplified;
4383 return CI->use_empty() ? CI : replaceInstUsesWith(*CI, With);
4384 }
4385 if (Value *With = optimizeModularFormat(CI, Builder)) {
4386 ++NumSimplified;
4387 return CI->use_empty() ? CI : replaceInstUsesWith(*CI, With);
4388 }
4389
4390 return nullptr;
4391}
4392
4393static IntrinsicInst *findInitTrampolineFromAlloca(Value *TrampMem) {
4394 // Strip off at most one level of pointer casts, looking for an alloca. This
4395 // is good enough in practice and simpler than handling any number of casts.
4396 Value *Underlying = TrampMem->stripPointerCasts();
4397 if (Underlying != TrampMem &&
4398 (!Underlying->hasOneUse() || Underlying->user_back() != TrampMem))
4399 return nullptr;
4400 if (!isa<AllocaInst>(Underlying))
4401 return nullptr;
4402
4403 IntrinsicInst *InitTrampoline = nullptr;
4404 for (User *U : TrampMem->users()) {
4405 IntrinsicInst *II = dyn_cast<IntrinsicInst>(U);
4406 if (!II)
4407 return nullptr;
4408 if (II->getIntrinsicID() == Intrinsic::init_trampoline) {
4409 if (InitTrampoline)
4410 // More than one init_trampoline writes to this value. Give up.
4411 return nullptr;
4412 InitTrampoline = II;
4413 continue;
4414 }
4415 if (II->getIntrinsicID() == Intrinsic::adjust_trampoline)
4416 // Allow any number of calls to adjust.trampoline.
4417 continue;
4418 return nullptr;
4419 }
4420
4421 // No call to init.trampoline found.
4422 if (!InitTrampoline)
4423 return nullptr;
4424
4425 // Check that the alloca is being used in the expected way.
4426 if (InitTrampoline->getOperand(0) != TrampMem)
4427 return nullptr;
4428
4429 return InitTrampoline;
4430}
4431
4432static IntrinsicInst *findInitTrampolineFromBB(IntrinsicInst *AdjustTramp,
4433 Value *TrampMem) {
4434 // Visit all the previous instructions in the basic block, and try to find a
4435 // init.trampoline which has a direct path to the adjust.trampoline.
4436 for (BasicBlock::iterator I = AdjustTramp->getIterator(),
4437 E = AdjustTramp->getParent()->begin();
4438 I != E;) {
4439 Instruction *Inst = &*--I;
4440 if (IntrinsicInst *II = dyn_cast<IntrinsicInst>(I))
4441 if (II->getIntrinsicID() == Intrinsic::init_trampoline &&
4442 II->getOperand(0) == TrampMem)
4443 return II;
4444 if (Inst->mayWriteToMemory())
4445 return nullptr;
4446 }
4447 return nullptr;
4448}
4449
4450// Given a call to llvm.adjust.trampoline, find and return the corresponding
4451// call to llvm.init.trampoline if the call to the trampoline can be optimized
4452// to a direct call to a function. Otherwise return NULL.
4453static IntrinsicInst *findInitTrampoline(Value *Callee) {
4454 Callee = Callee->stripPointerCasts();
4455 IntrinsicInst *AdjustTramp = dyn_cast<IntrinsicInst>(Callee);
4456 if (!AdjustTramp ||
4457 AdjustTramp->getIntrinsicID() != Intrinsic::adjust_trampoline)
4458 return nullptr;
4459
4460 Value *TrampMem = AdjustTramp->getOperand(0);
4461
4462 if (IntrinsicInst *IT = findInitTrampolineFromAlloca(TrampMem))
4463 return IT;
4464 if (IntrinsicInst *IT = findInitTrampolineFromBB(AdjustTramp, TrampMem))
4465 return IT;
4466 return nullptr;
4467}
4468
4469Instruction *InstCombinerImpl::foldPtrAuthIntrinsicCallee(CallBase &Call) {
4470 const Value *Callee = Call.getCalledOperand();
4471 const auto *IPC = dyn_cast<IntToPtrInst>(Callee);
4472 if (!IPC || !IPC->isNoopCast(DL))
4473 return nullptr;
4474
4475 const auto *II = dyn_cast<IntrinsicInst>(IPC->getOperand(0));
4476 if (!II)
4477 return nullptr;
4478
4479 Intrinsic::ID IIID = II->getIntrinsicID();
4480 if (IIID != Intrinsic::ptrauth_resign && IIID != Intrinsic::ptrauth_sign)
4481 return nullptr;
4482
4483 // Isolate the ptrauth bundle from the others.
4484 std::optional<OperandBundleUse> PtrAuthBundleOrNone;
4485 SmallVector<OperandBundleDef, 2> NewBundles;
4486 for (unsigned BI = 0, BE = Call.getNumOperandBundles(); BI != BE; ++BI) {
4487 OperandBundleUse Bundle = Call.getOperandBundleAt(BI);
4488 if (Bundle.getTagID() == LLVMContext::OB_ptrauth)
4489 PtrAuthBundleOrNone = Bundle;
4490 else
4491 NewBundles.emplace_back(Bundle);
4492 }
4493
4494 if (!PtrAuthBundleOrNone)
4495 return nullptr;
4496
4497 Value *NewCallee = nullptr;
4498 switch (IIID) {
4499 // call(ptrauth.resign(p)), ["ptrauth"()] -> call p, ["ptrauth"()]
4500 // assuming the call bundle and the sign operands match.
4501 case Intrinsic::ptrauth_resign: {
4502 // Resign result key should match bundle.
4503 if (II->getOperand(3) != PtrAuthBundleOrNone->Inputs[0])
4504 return nullptr;
4505 // Resign result discriminator should match bundle.
4506 if (II->getOperand(4) != PtrAuthBundleOrNone->Inputs[1])
4507 return nullptr;
4508
4509 // Resign input (auth) key should also match: we can't change the key on
4510 // the new call we're generating, because we don't know what keys are valid.
4511 if (II->getOperand(1) != PtrAuthBundleOrNone->Inputs[0])
4512 return nullptr;
4513
4514 Value *NewBundleOps[] = {II->getOperand(1), II->getOperand(2)};
4515 NewBundles.emplace_back("ptrauth", NewBundleOps);
4516 NewCallee = II->getOperand(0);
4517 break;
4518 }
4519
4520 // call(ptrauth.sign(p)), ["ptrauth"()] -> call p
4521 // assuming the call bundle and the sign operands match.
4522 // Non-ptrauth indirect calls are undesirable, but so is ptrauth.sign.
4523 case Intrinsic::ptrauth_sign: {
4524 // Sign key should match bundle.
4525 if (II->getOperand(1) != PtrAuthBundleOrNone->Inputs[0])
4526 return nullptr;
4527 // Sign discriminator should match bundle.
4528 if (II->getOperand(2) != PtrAuthBundleOrNone->Inputs[1])
4529 return nullptr;
4530 NewCallee = II->getOperand(0);
4531 break;
4532 }
4533 default:
4534 llvm_unreachable("unexpected intrinsic ID");
4535 }
4536
4537 if (!NewCallee)
4538 return nullptr;
4539
4540 NewCallee = Builder.CreateBitOrPointerCast(NewCallee, Callee->getType());
4541 CallBase *NewCall = CallBase::Create(&Call, NewBundles);
4542 NewCall->setCalledOperand(NewCallee);
4543 return NewCall;
4544}
4545
4546Instruction *InstCombinerImpl::foldPtrAuthConstantCallee(CallBase &Call) {
4547 auto *CPA = dyn_cast<ConstantPtrAuth>(Call.getCalledOperand());
4548 if (!CPA)
4549 return nullptr;
4550
4551 auto *CalleeF = dyn_cast<Function>(CPA->getPointer());
4552 // If the ptrauth constant isn't based on a function pointer, bail out.
4553 if (!CalleeF)
4554 return nullptr;
4555
4556 // Inspect the call ptrauth bundle to check it matches the ptrauth constant.
4557 auto PAB = Call.getOperandBundle(LLVMContext::OB_ptrauth);
4558 if (!PAB)
4559 return nullptr;
4560
4561 auto *Key = cast<ConstantInt>(PAB->Inputs[0]);
4562 Value *Discriminator = PAB->Inputs[1];
4563
4564 // If the bundle doesn't match, this is probably going to fail to auth.
4565 if (!CPA->isKnownCompatibleWith(Key, Discriminator, DL))
4566 return nullptr;
4567
4568 // If the bundle matches the constant, proceed in making this a direct call.
4569 auto *NewCall = CallBase::removeOperandBundle(&Call, LLVMContext::OB_ptrauth);
4570 NewCall->setCalledOperand(CalleeF);
4571 return NewCall;
4572}
4573
4574bool InstCombinerImpl::annotateAnyAllocSite(CallBase &Call,
4575 const TargetLibraryInfo *TLI) {
4576 // Note: We only handle cases which can't be driven from generic attributes
4577 // here. So, for example, nonnull and noalias (which are common properties
4578 // of some allocation functions) are expected to be handled via annotation
4579 // of the respective allocator declaration with generic attributes.
4580 bool Changed = false;
4581
4582 if (!Call.getType()->isPointerTy())
4583 return Changed;
4584
4585 std::optional<APInt> Size = getAllocSize(&Call, TLI);
4586 if (Size && *Size != 0) {
4587 // TODO: We really should just emit deref_or_null here and then
4588 // let the generic inference code combine that with nonnull.
4589 if (Call.hasRetAttr(Attribute::NonNull)) {
4590 Changed = !Call.hasRetAttr(Attribute::Dereferenceable);
4591 Call.addRetAttr(Attribute::getWithDereferenceableBytes(
4592 Call.getContext(), Size->getLimitedValue()));
4593 } else {
4594 Changed = !Call.hasRetAttr(Attribute::DereferenceableOrNull);
4595 Call.addRetAttr(Attribute::getWithDereferenceableOrNullBytes(
4596 Call.getContext(), Size->getLimitedValue()));
4597 }
4598 }
4599
4600 // Add alignment attribute if alignment is a power of two constant.
4601 Value *Alignment = getAllocAlignment(&Call, TLI);
4602 if (!Alignment)
4603 return Changed;
4604
4605 ConstantInt *AlignOpC = dyn_cast<ConstantInt>(Alignment);
4606 if (AlignOpC && AlignOpC->getValue().ult(llvm::Value::MaximumAlignment)) {
4607 uint64_t AlignmentVal = AlignOpC->getZExtValue();
4608 if (llvm::isPowerOf2_64(AlignmentVal)) {
4609 Align ExistingAlign = Call.getRetAlign().valueOrOne();
4610 Align NewAlign = Align(AlignmentVal);
4611 if (NewAlign > ExistingAlign) {
4612 Call.addRetAttr(
4613 Attribute::getWithAlignment(Call.getContext(), NewAlign));
4614 Changed = true;
4615 }
4616 }
4617 }
4618 return Changed;
4619}
4620
4621/// Improvements for call, callbr and invoke instructions.
4622Instruction *InstCombinerImpl::visitCallBase(CallBase &Call) {
4623 bool Changed = annotateAnyAllocSite(Call, &TLI);
4624
4625 // Mark any parameters that are known to be non-null with the nonnull
4626 // attribute. This is helpful for inlining calls to functions with null
4627 // checks on their arguments.
4628 SmallVector<unsigned, 4> ArgNos;
4629 unsigned ArgNo = 0;
4630
4631 for (Value *V : Call.args()) {
4632 if (V->getType()->isPointerTy()) {
4633 // Simplify the nonnull operand if the parameter is known to be nonnull.
4634 // Otherwise, try to infer nonnull for it.
4635 bool HasDereferenceable = Call.getParamDereferenceableBytes(ArgNo) > 0;
4636 if (Call.paramHasAttr(ArgNo, Attribute::NonNull) ||
4637 (HasDereferenceable &&
4638 !NullPointerIsDefined(Call.getFunction(),
4639 V->getType()->getPointerAddressSpace()))) {
4640 if (Value *Res = simplifyNonNullOperand(V, HasDereferenceable)) {
4641 replaceOperand(Call, ArgNo, Res);
4642 Changed = true;
4643 }
4644 } else if (isKnownNonZero(V,
4645 getSimplifyQuery().getWithInstruction(&Call))) {
4646 ArgNos.push_back(ArgNo);
4647 }
4648 }
4649 ArgNo++;
4650 }
4651
4652 assert(ArgNo == Call.arg_size() && "Call arguments not processed correctly.");
4653
4654 if (!ArgNos.empty()) {
4655 AttributeList AS = Call.getAttributes();
4656 LLVMContext &Ctx = Call.getContext();
4657 AS = AS.addParamAttribute(Ctx, ArgNos,
4658 Attribute::get(Ctx, Attribute::NonNull));
4659 Call.setAttributes(AS);
4660 Changed = true;
4661 }
4662
4663 // If the callee is a pointer to a function, attempt to move any casts to the
4664 // arguments of the call/callbr/invoke.
4665 Value *Callee = Call.getCalledOperand();
4666 Function *CalleeF = dyn_cast<Function>(Callee);
4667 if ((!CalleeF || CalleeF->getFunctionType() != Call.getFunctionType()) &&
4668 transformConstExprCastCall(Call))
4669 return nullptr;
4670
4671 if (CalleeF) {
4672 // Remove the convergent attr on calls when the callee is not convergent.
4673 if (Call.isConvergent() && !CalleeF->isConvergent() &&
4674 !CalleeF->isIntrinsic()) {
4675 LLVM_DEBUG(dbgs() << "Removing convergent attr from instr " << Call
4676 << "\n");
4677 Call.setNotConvergent();
4678 return &Call;
4679 }
4680
4681 // If the call and callee calling conventions don't match, and neither one
4682 // of the calling conventions is compatible with C calling convention
4683 // this call must be unreachable, as the call is undefined.
4684 if ((CalleeF->getCallingConv() != Call.getCallingConv() &&
4685 !(CalleeF->getCallingConv() == llvm::CallingConv::C &&
4686 TargetLibraryInfoImpl::isCallingConvCCompatible(&Call)) &&
4687 !(Call.getCallingConv() == llvm::CallingConv::C &&
4688 TargetLibraryInfoImpl::isCallingConvCCompatible(CalleeF))) &&
4689 // Only do this for calls to a function with a body. A prototype may
4690 // not actually end up matching the implementation's calling conv for a
4691 // variety of reasons (e.g. it may be written in assembly).
4692 !CalleeF->isDeclaration()) {
4693 Instruction *OldCall = &Call;
4694 CreateNonTerminatorUnreachable(OldCall);
4695 // If OldCall does not return void then replaceInstUsesWith poison.
4696 // This allows ValueHandlers and custom metadata to adjust itself.
4697 if (!OldCall->getType()->isVoidTy())
4698 replaceInstUsesWith(*OldCall, PoisonValue::get(OldCall->getType()));
4699 if (isa<CallInst>(OldCall))
4700 return eraseInstFromFunction(*OldCall);
4701
4702 // We cannot remove an invoke or a callbr, because it would change thexi
4703 // CFG, just change the callee to a null pointer.
4704 cast<CallBase>(OldCall)->setCalledFunction(
4705 CalleeF->getFunctionType(),
4706 Constant::getNullValue(CalleeF->getType()));
4707 return nullptr;
4708 }
4709 }
4710
4711 // Calling a null function pointer is undefined if a null address isn't
4712 // dereferenceable.
4713 if ((isa<ConstantPointerNull>(Callee) &&
4714 !NullPointerIsDefined(Call.getFunction())) ||
4715 isa<UndefValue>(Callee)) {
4716 // If Call does not return void then replaceInstUsesWith poison.
4717 // This allows ValueHandlers and custom metadata to adjust itself.
4718 if (!Call.getType()->isVoidTy())
4719 replaceInstUsesWith(Call, PoisonValue::get(Call.getType()));
4720
4721 if (Call.isTerminator()) {
4722 // Can't remove an invoke or callbr because we cannot change the CFG.
4723 return nullptr;
4724 }
4725
4726 // This instruction is not reachable, just remove it.
4727 CreateNonTerminatorUnreachable(&Call);
4728 return eraseInstFromFunction(Call);
4729 }
4730
4731 if (IntrinsicInst *II = findInitTrampoline(Callee))
4732 return transformCallThroughTrampoline(Call, *II);
4733
4734 // Combine calls involving pointer authentication intrinsics.
4735 if (Instruction *NewCall = foldPtrAuthIntrinsicCallee(Call))
4736 return NewCall;
4737
4738 // Combine calls to ptrauth constants.
4739 if (Instruction *NewCall = foldPtrAuthConstantCallee(Call))
4740 return NewCall;
4741
4742 if (isa<InlineAsm>(Callee) && !Call.doesNotThrow()) {
4743 InlineAsm *IA = cast<InlineAsm>(Callee);
4744 if (!IA->canThrow()) {
4745 // Normal inline asm calls cannot throw - mark them
4746 // 'nounwind'.
4747 Call.setDoesNotThrow();
4748 Changed = true;
4749 }
4750 }
4751
4752 // Try to optimize the call if possible, we require DataLayout for most of
4753 // this. None of these calls are seen as possibly dead so go ahead and
4754 // delete the instruction now.
4755 if (CallInst *CI = dyn_cast<CallInst>(&Call)) {
4756 Instruction *I = tryOptimizeCall(CI);
4757 // If we changed something return the result, etc. Otherwise let
4758 // the fallthrough check.
4759 if (I) return eraseInstFromFunction(*I);
4760 }
4761
4762 if (!Call.use_empty() && !Call.isMustTailCall())
4763 if (Value *ReturnedArg = Call.getReturnedArgOperand()) {
4764 Type *CallTy = Call.getType();
4765 Type *RetArgTy = ReturnedArg->getType();
4766 if (RetArgTy->canLosslesslyBitCastTo(CallTy))
4767 return replaceInstUsesWith(
4768 Call, Builder.CreateBitOrPointerCast(ReturnedArg, CallTy));
4769 }
4770
4771 // Drop unnecessary callee_type metadata from calls that were converted
4772 // into direct calls.
4773 if (Call.getMetadata(LLVMContext::MD_callee_type) && !Call.isIndirectCall()) {
4774 Call.setMetadata(LLVMContext::MD_callee_type, nullptr);
4775 Changed = true;
4776 }
4777
4778 // Drop unnecessary kcfi operand bundles from calls that were converted
4779 // into direct calls.
4780 auto Bundle = Call.getOperandBundle(LLVMContext::OB_kcfi);
4781 if (Bundle && !Call.isIndirectCall()) {
4782 DEBUG_WITH_TYPE(DEBUG_TYPE "-kcfi", {
4783 if (CalleeF) {
4784 ConstantInt *FunctionType = nullptr;
4785 ConstantInt *ExpectedType = cast<ConstantInt>(Bundle->Inputs[0]);
4786
4787 if (MDNode *MD = CalleeF->getMetadata(LLVMContext::MD_kcfi_type))
4788 FunctionType = mdconst::extract<ConstantInt>(MD->getOperand(0));
4789
4790 if (FunctionType &&
4791 FunctionType->getZExtValue() != ExpectedType->getZExtValue())
4792 dbgs() << Call.getModule()->getName()
4793 << ": warning: kcfi: " << Call.getCaller()->getName()
4794 << ": call to " << CalleeF->getName()
4795 << " using a mismatching function pointer type\n";
4796 }
4797 });
4798
4799 return CallBase::removeOperandBundle(&Call, LLVMContext::OB_kcfi);
4800 }
4801
4802 if (isRemovableAlloc(&Call, &TLI))
4803 return visitAllocSite(Call);
4804
4805 // Handle intrinsics which can be used in both call and invoke context.
4806 switch (Call.getIntrinsicID()) {
4807 case Intrinsic::experimental_gc_statepoint: {
4808 GCStatepointInst &GCSP = *cast<GCStatepointInst>(&Call);
4809 SmallPtrSet<Value *, 32> LiveGcValues;
4810 for (const GCRelocateInst *Reloc : GCSP.getGCRelocates()) {
4811 GCRelocateInst &GCR = *const_cast<GCRelocateInst *>(Reloc);
4812
4813 // Remove the relocation if unused.
4814 if (GCR.use_empty()) {
4815 eraseInstFromFunction(GCR);
4816 continue;
4817 }
4818
4819 Value *DerivedPtr = GCR.getDerivedPtr();
4820 Value *BasePtr = GCR.getBasePtr();
4821
4822 // Undef is undef, even after relocation.
4823 if (isa<UndefValue>(DerivedPtr) || isa<UndefValue>(BasePtr)) {
4824 replaceInstUsesWith(GCR, UndefValue::get(GCR.getType()));
4825 eraseInstFromFunction(GCR);
4826 continue;
4827 }
4828
4829 if (auto *PT = dyn_cast<PointerType>(GCR.getType())) {
4830 // The relocation of null will be null for most any collector.
4831 // TODO: provide a hook for this in GCStrategy. There might be some
4832 // weird collector this property does not hold for.
4833 if (isa<ConstantPointerNull>(DerivedPtr)) {
4834 // Use null-pointer of gc_relocate's type to replace it.
4835 replaceInstUsesWith(GCR, ConstantPointerNull::get(PT));
4836 eraseInstFromFunction(GCR);
4837 continue;
4838 }
4839
4840 // isKnownNonNull -> nonnull attribute
4841 if (!GCR.hasRetAttr(Attribute::NonNull) &&
4842 isKnownNonZero(DerivedPtr,
4843 getSimplifyQuery().getWithInstruction(&Call))) {
4844 GCR.addRetAttr(Attribute::NonNull);
4845 // We discovered new fact, re-check users.
4846 Worklist.pushUsersToWorkList(GCR);
4847 }
4848 }
4849
4850 // If we have two copies of the same pointer in the statepoint argument
4851 // list, canonicalize to one. This may let us common gc.relocates.
4852 if (GCR.getBasePtr() == GCR.getDerivedPtr() &&
4853 GCR.getBasePtrIndex() != GCR.getDerivedPtrIndex()) {
4854 auto *OpIntTy = GCR.getOperand(2)->getType();
4855 GCR.setOperand(2, ConstantInt::get(OpIntTy, GCR.getBasePtrIndex()));
4856 }
4857
4858 // TODO: bitcast(relocate(p)) -> relocate(bitcast(p))
4859 // Canonicalize on the type from the uses to the defs
4860
4861 // TODO: relocate((gep p, C, C2, ...)) -> gep(relocate(p), C, C2, ...)
4862 LiveGcValues.insert(BasePtr);
4863 LiveGcValues.insert(DerivedPtr);
4864 }
4865 std::optional<OperandBundleUse> Bundle =
4866 GCSP.getOperandBundle(LLVMContext::OB_gc_live);
4867 unsigned NumOfGCLives = LiveGcValues.size();
4868 if (!Bundle || NumOfGCLives == Bundle->Inputs.size())
4869 break;
4870 // We can reduce the size of gc live bundle.
4871 DenseMap<Value *, unsigned> Val2Idx;
4872 std::vector<Value *> NewLiveGc;
4873 for (Value *V : Bundle->Inputs) {
4874 auto [It, Inserted] = Val2Idx.try_emplace(V);
4875 if (!Inserted)
4876 continue;
4877 if (LiveGcValues.count(V)) {
4878 It->second = NewLiveGc.size();
4879 NewLiveGc.push_back(V);
4880 } else
4881 It->second = NumOfGCLives;
4882 }
4883 // Update all gc.relocates
4884 for (const GCRelocateInst *Reloc : GCSP.getGCRelocates()) {
4885 GCRelocateInst &GCR = *const_cast<GCRelocateInst *>(Reloc);
4886 Value *BasePtr = GCR.getBasePtr();
4887 assert(Val2Idx.count(BasePtr) && Val2Idx[BasePtr] != NumOfGCLives &&
4888 "Missed live gc for base pointer");
4889 auto *OpIntTy1 = GCR.getOperand(1)->getType();
4890 GCR.setOperand(1, ConstantInt::get(OpIntTy1, Val2Idx[BasePtr]));
4891 Value *DerivedPtr = GCR.getDerivedPtr();
4892 assert(Val2Idx.count(DerivedPtr) && Val2Idx[DerivedPtr] != NumOfGCLives &&
4893 "Missed live gc for derived pointer");
4894 auto *OpIntTy2 = GCR.getOperand(2)->getType();
4895 GCR.setOperand(2, ConstantInt::get(OpIntTy2, Val2Idx[DerivedPtr]));
4896 }
4897 // Create new statepoint instruction.
4898 OperandBundleDef NewBundle("gc-live", NewLiveGc);
4899 return CallBase::Create(&Call, NewBundle);
4900 }
4901 default: { break; }
4902 }
4903
4904 return Changed ? &Call : nullptr;
4905}
4906
4907/// If the callee is a constexpr cast of a function, attempt to move the cast to
4908/// the arguments of the call/invoke.
4909/// CallBrInst is not supported.
4910bool InstCombinerImpl::transformConstExprCastCall(CallBase &Call) {
4911 auto *Callee =
4912 dyn_cast<Function>(Call.getCalledOperand()->stripPointerCasts());
4913 if (!Callee)
4914 return false;
4915
4916 assert(!isa<CallBrInst>(Call) &&
4917 "CallBr's don't have a single point after a def to insert at");
4918
4919 // Don't perform the transform for declarations, which may not be fully
4920 // accurate. For example, void @foo() is commonly used as a placeholder for
4921 // unknown prototypes.
4922 if (Callee->isDeclaration())
4923 return false;
4924
4925 // If this is a call to a thunk function, don't remove the cast. Thunks are
4926 // used to transparently forward all incoming parameters and outgoing return
4927 // values, so it's important to leave the cast in place.
4928 if (Callee->hasFnAttribute("thunk"))
4929 return false;
4930
4931 // If this is a call to a naked function, the assembly might be
4932 // using an argument, or otherwise rely on the frame layout,
4933 // the function prototype will mismatch.
4934 if (Callee->hasFnAttribute(Attribute::Naked))
4935 return false;
4936
4937 // If this is a musttail call, the callee's prototype must match the caller's
4938 // prototype with the exception of pointee types. The code below doesn't
4939 // implement that, so we can't do this transform.
4940 // TODO: Do the transform if it only requires adding pointer casts.
4941 if (Call.isMustTailCall())
4942 return false;
4943
4944 Instruction *Caller = &Call;
4945 const AttributeList &CallerPAL = Call.getAttributes();
4946
4947 // Okay, this is a cast from a function to a different type. Unless doing so
4948 // would cause a type conversion of one of our arguments, change this call to
4949 // be a direct call with arguments casted to the appropriate types.
4950 FunctionType *FT = Callee->getFunctionType();
4951 Type *OldRetTy = Caller->getType();
4952 Type *NewRetTy = FT->getReturnType();
4953
4954 // Check to see if we are changing the return type...
4955 if (OldRetTy != NewRetTy) {
4956
4957 if (NewRetTy->isStructTy())
4958 return false; // TODO: Handle multiple return values.
4959
4960 if (!CastInst::isBitOrNoopPointerCastable(NewRetTy, OldRetTy, DL)) {
4961 if (!Caller->use_empty())
4962 return false; // Cannot transform this return value.
4963 }
4964
4965 if (!CallerPAL.isEmpty() && !Caller->use_empty()) {
4966 AttrBuilder RAttrs(FT->getContext(), CallerPAL.getRetAttrs());
4967 if (RAttrs.overlaps(AttributeFuncs::typeIncompatible(
4968 NewRetTy, CallerPAL.getRetAttrs())))
4969 return false; // Attribute not compatible with transformed value.
4970 }
4971
4972 // If the callbase is an invoke instruction, and the return value is
4973 // used by a PHI node in a successor, we cannot change the return type of
4974 // the call because there is no place to put the cast instruction (without
4975 // breaking the critical edge). Bail out in this case.
4976 if (!Caller->use_empty()) {
4977 BasicBlock *PhisNotSupportedBlock = nullptr;
4978 if (auto *II = dyn_cast<InvokeInst>(Caller))
4979 PhisNotSupportedBlock = II->getNormalDest();
4980 if (PhisNotSupportedBlock)
4981 for (User *U : Caller->users())
4982 if (PHINode *PN = dyn_cast<PHINode>(U))
4983 if (PN->getParent() == PhisNotSupportedBlock)
4984 return false;
4985 }
4986 }
4987
4988 unsigned NumActualArgs = Call.arg_size();
4989 unsigned NumCommonArgs = std::min(FT->getNumParams(), NumActualArgs);
4990
4991 // Prevent us turning:
4992 // declare void @takes_i32_inalloca(i32* inalloca)
4993 // call void bitcast (void (i32*)* @takes_i32_inalloca to void (i32)*)(i32 0)
4994 //
4995 // into:
4996 // call void @takes_i32_inalloca(i32* null)
4997 //
4998 // Similarly, avoid folding away bitcasts of byval calls.
4999 if (Callee->getAttributes().hasAttrSomewhere(Attribute::InAlloca) ||
5000 Callee->getAttributes().hasAttrSomewhere(Attribute::Preallocated))
5001 return false;
5002
5003 auto AI = Call.arg_begin();
5004 for (unsigned i = 0, e = NumCommonArgs; i != e; ++i, ++AI) {
5005 Type *ParamTy = FT->getParamType(i);
5006 Type *ActTy = (*AI)->getType();
5007
5008 if (!CastInst::isBitOrNoopPointerCastable(ActTy, ParamTy, DL))
5009 return false; // Cannot transform this parameter value.
5010
5011 // Check if there are any incompatible attributes we cannot drop safely.
5012 if (AttrBuilder(FT->getContext(), CallerPAL.getParamAttrs(i))
5013 .overlaps(AttributeFuncs::typeIncompatible(
5014 ParamTy, CallerPAL.getParamAttrs(i),
5015 AttributeFuncs::ASK_UNSAFE_TO_DROP)))
5016 return false; // Attribute not compatible with transformed value.
5017
5018 if (Call.isInAllocaArgument(i) ||
5019 CallerPAL.hasParamAttr(i, Attribute::Preallocated))
5020 return false; // Cannot transform to and from inalloca/preallocated.
5021
5022 if (CallerPAL.hasParamAttr(i, Attribute::SwiftError))
5023 return false;
5024
5025 if (CallerPAL.hasParamAttr(i, Attribute::ByVal) !=
5026 Callee->getAttributes().hasParamAttr(i, Attribute::ByVal))
5027 return false; // Cannot transform to or from byval.
5028 }
5029
5030 if (FT->getNumParams() < NumActualArgs && FT->isVarArg() &&
5031 !CallerPAL.isEmpty()) {
5032 // In this case we have more arguments than the new function type, but we
5033 // won't be dropping them. Check that these extra arguments have attributes
5034 // that are compatible with being a vararg call argument.
5035 unsigned SRetIdx;
5036 if (CallerPAL.hasAttrSomewhere(Attribute::StructRet, &SRetIdx) &&
5037 SRetIdx - AttributeList::FirstArgIndex >= FT->getNumParams())
5038 return false;
5039 }
5040
5041 // Okay, we decided that this is a safe thing to do: go ahead and start
5042 // inserting cast instructions as necessary.
5043 SmallVector<Value *, 8> Args;
5044 SmallVector<AttributeSet, 8> ArgAttrs;
5045 Args.reserve(NumActualArgs);
5046 ArgAttrs.reserve(NumActualArgs);
5047
5048 // Get any return attributes.
5049 AttrBuilder RAttrs(FT->getContext(), CallerPAL.getRetAttrs());
5050
5051 // If the return value is not being used, the type may not be compatible
5052 // with the existing attributes. Wipe out any problematic attributes.
5053 RAttrs.remove(
5054 AttributeFuncs::typeIncompatible(NewRetTy, CallerPAL.getRetAttrs()));
5055
5056 LLVMContext &Ctx = Call.getContext();
5057 AI = Call.arg_begin();
5058 for (unsigned i = 0; i != NumCommonArgs; ++i, ++AI) {
5059 Type *ParamTy = FT->getParamType(i);
5060
5061 Value *NewArg = *AI;
5062 if ((*AI)->getType() != ParamTy)
5063 NewArg = Builder.CreateBitOrPointerCast(*AI, ParamTy);
5064 Args.push_back(NewArg);
5065
5066 // Add any parameter attributes except the ones incompatible with the new
5067 // type. Note that we made sure all incompatible ones are safe to drop.
5068 AttributeMask IncompatibleAttrs = AttributeFuncs::typeIncompatible(
5069 ParamTy, CallerPAL.getParamAttrs(i), AttributeFuncs::ASK_SAFE_TO_DROP);
5070 ArgAttrs.push_back(
5071 CallerPAL.getParamAttrs(i).removeAttributes(Ctx, IncompatibleAttrs));
5072 }
5073
5074 // If the function takes more arguments than the call was taking, add them
5075 // now.
5076 for (unsigned i = NumCommonArgs; i != FT->getNumParams(); ++i) {
5077 Args.push_back(Constant::getNullValue(FT->getParamType(i)));
5078 ArgAttrs.push_back(AttributeSet());
5079 }
5080
5081 // If we are removing arguments to the function, emit an obnoxious warning.
5082 if (FT->getNumParams() < NumActualArgs) {
5083 // TODO: if (!FT->isVarArg()) this call may be unreachable. PR14722
5084 if (FT->isVarArg()) {
5085 // Add all of the arguments in their promoted form to the arg list.
5086 for (unsigned i = FT->getNumParams(); i != NumActualArgs; ++i, ++AI) {
5087 Type *PTy = getPromotedType((*AI)->getType());
5088 Value *NewArg = *AI;
5089 if (PTy != (*AI)->getType()) {
5090 // Must promote to pass through va_arg area!
5091 Instruction::CastOps opcode =
5092 CastInst::getCastOpcode(*AI, false, PTy, false);
5093 NewArg = Builder.CreateCast(opcode, *AI, PTy);
5094 }
5095 Args.push_back(NewArg);
5096
5097 // Add any parameter attributes.
5098 ArgAttrs.push_back(CallerPAL.getParamAttrs(i));
5099 }
5100 }
5101 }
5102
5103 AttributeSet FnAttrs = CallerPAL.getFnAttrs();
5104
5105 if (NewRetTy->isVoidTy())
5106 Caller->setName(""); // Void type should not have a name.
5107
5108 assert((ArgAttrs.size() == FT->getNumParams() || FT->isVarArg()) &&
5109 "missing argument attributes");
5110 AttributeList NewCallerPAL = AttributeList::get(
5111 Ctx, FnAttrs, AttributeSet::get(Ctx, RAttrs), ArgAttrs);
5112
5113 SmallVector<OperandBundleDef, 1> OpBundles;
5114 Call.getOperandBundlesAsDefs(OpBundles);
5115
5116 CallBase *NewCall;
5117 if (InvokeInst *II = dyn_cast<InvokeInst>(Caller)) {
5118 NewCall = Builder.CreateInvoke(Callee, II->getNormalDest(),
5119 II->getUnwindDest(), Args, OpBundles);
5120 } else {
5121 NewCall = Builder.CreateCall(Callee, Args, OpBundles);
5122 cast<CallInst>(NewCall)->setTailCallKind(
5123 cast<CallInst>(Caller)->getTailCallKind());
5124 }
5125 NewCall->takeName(Caller);
5126 NewCall->setCallingConv(Call.getCallingConv());
5127 NewCall->setAttributes(NewCallerPAL);
5128
5129 // Preserve prof metadata if any.
5130 NewCall->copyMetadata(*Caller, {LLVMContext::MD_prof});
5131
5132 // Insert a cast of the return type as necessary.
5133 Instruction *NC = NewCall;
5134 Value *NV = NC;
5135 if (OldRetTy != NV->getType() && !Caller->use_empty()) {
5136 assert(!NV->getType()->isVoidTy());
5137 NV = NC = CastInst::CreateBitOrPointerCast(NC, OldRetTy);
5138 NC->setDebugLoc(Caller->getDebugLoc());
5139
5140 auto OptInsertPt = NewCall->getInsertionPointAfterDef();
5141 assert(OptInsertPt && "No place to insert cast");
5142 InsertNewInstBefore(NC, *OptInsertPt);
5143 Worklist.pushUsersToWorkList(*Caller);
5144 }
5145
5146 if (!Caller->use_empty())
5147 replaceInstUsesWith(*Caller, NV);
5148 else if (Caller->hasValueHandle()) {
5149 if (OldRetTy == NV->getType())
5150 ValueHandleBase::ValueIsRAUWd(Caller, NV);
5151 else
5152 // We cannot call ValueIsRAUWd with a different type, and the
5153 // actual tracked value will disappear.
5154 ValueHandleBase::ValueIsDeleted(Caller);
5155 }
5156
5157 eraseInstFromFunction(*Caller);
5158 return true;
5159}
5160
5161/// Turn a call to a function created by init_trampoline / adjust_trampoline
5162/// intrinsic pair into a direct call to the underlying function.
5163Instruction *
5164InstCombinerImpl::transformCallThroughTrampoline(CallBase &Call,
5165 IntrinsicInst &Tramp) {
5166 FunctionType *FTy = Call.getFunctionType();
5167 AttributeList Attrs = Call.getAttributes();
5168
5169 // If the call already has the 'nest' attribute somewhere then give up -
5170 // otherwise 'nest' would occur twice after splicing in the chain.
5171 if (Attrs.hasAttrSomewhere(Attribute::Nest))
5172 return nullptr;
5173
5174 Function *NestF = cast<Function>(Tramp.getArgOperand(1)->stripPointerCasts());
5175 FunctionType *NestFTy = NestF->getFunctionType();
5176
5177 AttributeList NestAttrs = NestF->getAttributes();
5178 if (!NestAttrs.isEmpty()) {
5179 unsigned NestArgNo = 0;
5180 Type *NestTy = nullptr;
5181 AttributeSet NestAttr;
5182
5183 // Look for a parameter marked with the 'nest' attribute.
5184 for (FunctionType::param_iterator I = NestFTy->param_begin(),
5185 E = NestFTy->param_end();
5186 I != E; ++NestArgNo, ++I) {
5187 AttributeSet AS = NestAttrs.getParamAttrs(NestArgNo);
5188 if (AS.hasAttribute(Attribute::Nest)) {
5189 // Record the parameter type and any other attributes.
5190 NestTy = *I;
5191 NestAttr = AS;
5192 break;
5193 }
5194 }
5195
5196 if (NestTy) {
5197 std::vector<Value*> NewArgs;
5198 std::vector<AttributeSet> NewArgAttrs;
5199 NewArgs.reserve(Call.arg_size() + 1);
5200 NewArgAttrs.reserve(Call.arg_size());
5201
5202 // Insert the nest argument into the call argument list, which may
5203 // mean appending it. Likewise for attributes.
5204
5205 {
5206 unsigned ArgNo = 0;
5207 auto I = Call.arg_begin(), E = Call.arg_end();
5208 do {
5209 if (ArgNo == NestArgNo) {
5210 // Add the chain argument and attributes.
5211 Value *NestVal = Tramp.getArgOperand(2);
5212 if (NestVal->getType() != NestTy)
5213 NestVal = Builder.CreateBitCast(NestVal, NestTy, "nest");
5214 NewArgs.push_back(NestVal);
5215 NewArgAttrs.push_back(NestAttr);
5216 }
5217
5218 if (I == E)
5219 break;
5220
5221 // Add the original argument and attributes.
5222 NewArgs.push_back(*I);
5223 NewArgAttrs.push_back(Attrs.getParamAttrs(ArgNo));
5224
5225 ++ArgNo;
5226 ++I;
5227 } while (true);
5228 }
5229
5230 // The trampoline may have been bitcast to a bogus type (FTy).
5231 // Handle this by synthesizing a new function type, equal to FTy
5232 // with the chain parameter inserted.
5233
5234 std::vector<Type*> NewTypes;
5235 NewTypes.reserve(FTy->getNumParams()+1);
5236
5237 // Insert the chain's type into the list of parameter types, which may
5238 // mean appending it.
5239 {
5240 unsigned ArgNo = 0;
5241 FunctionType::param_iterator I = FTy->param_begin(),
5242 E = FTy->param_end();
5243
5244 do {
5245 if (ArgNo == NestArgNo)
5246 // Add the chain's type.
5247 NewTypes.push_back(NestTy);
5248
5249 if (I == E)
5250 break;
5251
5252 // Add the original type.
5253 NewTypes.push_back(*I);
5254
5255 ++ArgNo;
5256 ++I;
5257 } while (true);
5258 }
5259
5260 // Replace the trampoline call with a direct call. Let the generic
5261 // code sort out any function type mismatches.
5262 FunctionType *NewFTy =
5263 FunctionType::get(FTy->getReturnType(), NewTypes, FTy->isVarArg());
5264 AttributeList NewPAL =
5265 AttributeList::get(FTy->getContext(), Attrs.getFnAttrs(),
5266 Attrs.getRetAttrs(), NewArgAttrs);
5267
5268 SmallVector<OperandBundleDef, 1> OpBundles;
5269 Call.getOperandBundlesAsDefs(OpBundles);
5270
5271 Instruction *NewCaller;
5272 if (InvokeInst *II = dyn_cast<InvokeInst>(&Call)) {
5273 NewCaller = InvokeInst::Create(NewFTy, NestF, II->getNormalDest(),
5274 II->getUnwindDest(), NewArgs, OpBundles);
5275 cast<InvokeInst>(NewCaller)->setCallingConv(II->getCallingConv());
5276 cast<InvokeInst>(NewCaller)->setAttributes(NewPAL);
5277 } else if (CallBrInst *CBI = dyn_cast<CallBrInst>(&Call)) {
5278 NewCaller =
5279 CallBrInst::Create(NewFTy, NestF, CBI->getDefaultDest(),
5280 CBI->getIndirectDests(), NewArgs, OpBundles);
5281 cast<CallBrInst>(NewCaller)->setCallingConv(CBI->getCallingConv());
5282 cast<CallBrInst>(NewCaller)->setAttributes(NewPAL);
5283 } else {
5284 NewCaller = CallInst::Create(NewFTy, NestF, NewArgs, OpBundles);
5285 cast<CallInst>(NewCaller)->setTailCallKind(
5286 cast<CallInst>(Call).getTailCallKind());
5287 cast<CallInst>(NewCaller)->setCallingConv(
5288 cast<CallInst>(Call).getCallingConv());
5289 cast<CallInst>(NewCaller)->setAttributes(NewPAL);
5290 }
5291 NewCaller->setDebugLoc(Call.getDebugLoc());
5292
5293 return NewCaller;
5294 }
5295 }
5296
5297 // Replace the trampoline call with a direct call. Since there is no 'nest'
5298 // parameter, there is no need to adjust the argument list. Let the generic
5299 // code sort out any function type mismatches.
5300 Call.setCalledFunction(FTy, NestF);
5301 return &Call;
5302}
